Title: OsLC1, a transaldolase, regulates cell patterning and leaf morphology through modulation of secondary metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pentose phosphate pathway linked to shikimate/phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Non-oxidative pentose phosphate pathway; precursor supply to shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the reversible transfer of a dihydroxyacetone unit between fructose-6-phosphate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form sedoheptulose-7-phosphate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for shikimate pathway and thereby supports downstream phenylpropanoid, lignin and flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignin provides structural support, cell wall integrity and defense; flavonoids contribute to auxin regulation and plant development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed across tissues, slightly higher in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seedling shoot and root; tiller-stage leaf and root; mature leaf, sheath, stem, panicle, section and root; strong GUS in vascular tissues and stomata
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsLC1 reduces E4P-derived secondary metabolism, decreasing lignin and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLC1 was map-cloned as Os01g0926300 and encodes a chloroplast transaldolase with in vitro and in planta TA activity. 2. Loss of OsLC1 increased S7P and decreased E4P, indicating altered metabolic flux in the non-oxidative PPP and reduced precursor supply to the shikimate pathway. 3. OsLC1 deficiency reduced lignin and flavonoid contents and downregulated multiple lignin/flavonoid biosynthetic genes. 4. These metabolic changes were linked to altered auxin homeostasis, abnormal leaf cellular patterning and rolled leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported as a rate-limiting/key enzyme in the conversion associated with PPP carbon flux; described by the paper as a key enzyme modulating metabolic flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; recombinant protein enzyme assay; in planta TA activity assay; metabolite measurement of S7P/E4P/lignin/flavonoids/IAA; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes rolled leaves, reduced plant height, tiller number, panicle length, seed setting rate, 1000-grain weight, photosynthesis and transpiration, and weaker drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for breeding to coordinate leaf architecture, yield and abiotic stress tolerance, but functional disruption causes strong agronomic penalties; engineering should preserve catalytic function while optimizing expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsLC1 promoter for GUS; Ubiquitin promoter for GFP localization; genomic complementation fragment containing native regulatory sequences
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High relative humidity (≥90% RH); exogenous IAA; 2,4-D; osmotic/drought-related conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OsLC1 is not a direct lignin/flavonoid biosynthetic enzyme, it is a core pathway gene because it enzymatically controls upstream carbon flux feeding these nutrient-related secondary metabolites.
